info.title: Lenz — Claim Verification API for AI Product Teams
info.description:
# The fact-check API for your LLM features

## Install the SDK

```bash
# Python
pip install lenz-io

# Node / TypeScript
npm install lenz-io
```

```python
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
v = client.verify_and_wait(claim="Sharks don't get cancer")
print(v.verdict.label, v.verdict.score)
# false 2.0
```

```typescript
import { Lenz } from "lenz-io";

const client = new Lenz({ apiKey: "lenz_..." });
const v = await client.verifyAndWait({ claim: "Sharks don't get cancer" });
console.log(v.verdict?.label, v.verdict?.score);
// false 2.0
```

Source on GitHub: [lenz-io-python](https://github.com/lenzhq/lenz-io-python) · [lenz-io-node](https://github.com/lenzhq/lenz-io-node).

## The two primitives

- **`POST /verify`** — verify one claim. Get back a truth score, confidence, and the sources behind it.
- **`POST /extract`** — pull verifiable claims out of any text (LLM output, article, transcript). Free, capped at 1000 calls/key/day.

Use them together — extract the claims your model emitted, then verify the ones that matter — or use either on its own.

## How it fits in your LLM pipeline

1. Your model emits an answer.
2. `POST /extract` returns the verifiable claims inside it.
3. `POST /verify` returns a verdict + sources for each claim that matters.
4. Surface, suppress, or flag the output. Your call.

## What's in the response

- A **`verdict`**: `label` (true / mostly true / false / unverified), `score` (0–10), `confidence`.
- **`sources`** — the evidence used, each with title, URL, snippet, and stance.
- An optional **`audit`** block — adjudication reasoning, debate transcript, panelist breakdowns, panel agreement.

## Why not just better RAG?

- **Independent verification** — your model can't grade its own output. Lenz is a separate panel with its own retrieval and reasoning.
- **Out-of-corpus claims** — RAG only knows what you indexed; we search the live web for current evidence.
- **Auditability** — every verdict ships with the sources and reasoning that produced it. Hand it to a compliance reviewer.

## How the API is organized

- **Verify** — submit a claim and poll status.
- **Verifications** — list, fetch, delete, and toggle visibility on your past verifications.
- **Follow-up** — ask follow-up questions on a verification.
- **Library** — browse the public catalog. No API key needed.

## Authentication

1. [Create a Lenz account](https://lenz.io/auth).
2. Generate an API key on the [API Integration](https://lenz.io/api-integration) page.
3. Send it on every request:
   ```
   Authorization: Bearer lenz_...
   ```

## Idempotency

Send an `Idempotency-Key` header on `POST /verify` and `POST /verify/batch` so retries after a network drop do not spawn duplicate tasks. The server caches the response body for 24h and returns it on replay; a re-used key with a different body returns 422. The SDKs auto-generate a key per `verify_and_wait` call by default.

## Webhooks

On `POST /verify` you may supply a `webhook_url`. We POST an HMAC-SHA256-signed JSON payload to it when the pipeline terminates. Signature header is `X-Lenz-Signature: sha256=<hex>`. Verify with your webhook secret (generate + rotate on [API Integration](https://lenz.io/api-integration)). Retries: 10s, 60s, 600s (3 retries after the initial delivery, 4 attempts total). The SDKs ship a `LenzWebhooks` helper that verifies signatures and parses the payload into typed events.

For commercial use, volume pricing, or onboarding support, [get in touch](https://lenz.io/contact).

get.description:
Search and browse the public catalog of verified claims.

Returns paginated results from the Lenz Library. Only publicly
published verifications are included.

**Sort options:** `recent`, `popular`, `most_true`, `most_untrue`,
`relevance` (only when `search` is provided).

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz()  # no api_key needed for the public library
page = client.library.list(page=1, sort="recent")
for v in page.items:
    print(v.claim, "→", v.verdict.label)

get.description:
Retrieve the full verification report for a single published item.

Returns the verdict, citations, and (under ``audit``) the panel
reasoning, debate transcript, and individual assessments.

post.description:
Submit a claim for verification.

Returns a ``task_id`` immediately. Poll ``GET /verify/status/{task_id}``
or supply ``webhook_url`` in the body for an asynchronous callback when
the pipeline terminates.

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
v = client.verify_and_wait(claim="Sharks don't get cancer")
print(v.verdict.label, v.verdict.score)
# false 2.0

post.description:
Fan-out wrapper around /verify for multi-claim LLM responses.

Body: ``{claims: [{text, source_url?, webhook_url?, visibility?}, ...]}``
capped at 20 items. Server pre-checks total credits, then fans out N
independent pipelines — each with its own ``task_id`` and lifecycle.
Each claim's webhook (per-item override, or batch ``webhook_url``, or
per-key default) fires when its pipeline terminates. ``batch_id`` is
purely for client-side correlation; the server doesn't gate anything
on it.

On a mid-fan-out enqueue error, the response includes the partial
``items`` list and a ``partial: true`` flag. Per-claim credit spend
happens at each claim's normal persist time — claims that never
enqueue are never charged.

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
batch = client.verify_batch(claims=[
    {"text": "The Eiffel Tower is in Berlin."},
    {"text": "Water boils at 100°C at sea level."},
])
for item in batch.items:
    print(item.task_id, item.claim_text)

post.description:
Extract verifiable claims from arbitrary text.

Runs only the framing step of the pipeline — no research, no debate,
no credit charge. Returns the list of verifiable claims, candidate
disambiguations (if the input was ambiguous), domain, key entities,
search queries, and presumed intent.

Free for API-key holders, capped at 1000 calls per key per day
(resets at 00:00 UTC). Keys with admin-set ``unlimited_extract=True``
bypass the cap.

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
claims = client.extract(text="Coffee causes cancer and the earth is flat.")
for c in claims.identified_claims:
    print(c)

post.description: Resolve a multi-claim interrupt — pick which claim to verify.

get.description: Poll processing status for a submitted verification.

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
status = client.get_status("tsk_abc123")
print(status.status)  # "processing" | "needs_input" | "completed" | "failed"

get.description:
Return the calling key's subscription credits and extract usage.

Shape::

    {
        "plan": "free" | "plus" | "pro",
        "credits_used": int,
        "credits_total": int,            # 0 if no monthly quota
        "credits_resets_at": iso8601 | null,
        "extract_calls_today": int,      # 0 for unlimited_extract keys
        "extract_daily_limit": int,
        "unlimited_extract": bool,
    }

Subscription credits track the per-claim quota that ``/verify`` and
``/verify/batch`` consume. ``extract_calls_today`` tracks the
per-key Redis counter that powers the daily ``/extract`` limit.

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
u = client.usage()
print(u.credits_used, "/", u.credits_total)

get.description: List the authenticated key's verifications (all visibilities).

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
page = client.verifications.list(page=1)
for v in page.items:
    print(v.verification_id, v.verdict.label)

get.description: Get full detail of an owned verification.

patch.description: Change the visibility of an owned verification.

get.description:
List public verifications semantically related to this one.

Returns the closest public-library claims by pgvector ANN distance
(cosine, 0 = identical). Excludes the verification itself, near-
duplicates (distance < 0.2), and editorially-hidden claims. Empty
list when the verification has no embedding yet (early pipeline) or
when no claim is close enough.

Access: any verification the caller owns OR any public library item.
``limit`` is clamped to [1, 10].

x-codeSamples.source:
from lenz_io import Lenz

client = Lenz(api_key="lenz_...")
related = client.verifications.related("vid_abc123", limit=5)
for r in related.items:
    print(r.verification_id, r.claim, r.verdict_label)

get.description: Get follow-up history and remaining quota for a verification.

delete.description: Delete all follow-up messages for this user on a verification.

post.description: Send a follow-up question and get an expert response.

EntityRef.description:
An entity (person, place, organization, concept) referenced in the
submitted claim.

``qid`` is the Wikidata Q identifier (e.g. ``Q42`` for Douglas Adams)
when Lenz resolved the entity against its internal catalog; ``null``
otherwise. Customers can join on ``qid`` to their own Wikidata-indexed
corpus, or hit the Wikidata API directly for richer metadata.
EntityRef.title: EntityRef

PublicLibraryItemOut.description: Single item in the public library listing.
PublicLibraryItemOut.title: PublicLibraryItemOut

VerdictOut.description: The single most-used block on a verification response.
VerdictOut.title: VerdictOut

AssessmentOut.description:
An adjudicator's individual assessment. Nested under ``audit``.

``warnings`` carries the panelist-specific flags (logical fallacies for
the Logic Examiner, missing context for the Context Analyst, weakest
sources for the Source Auditor). The kind is implicit in ``focus_area``;
every panelist emits exactly one category.
AssessmentOut.title: AssessmentOut

AuditOut.description:
Audit / explainability block — for callers that need to show their work.

``panel_agreement`` is derived from the panel: ``unanimous`` (all agree),
``majority`` (score spread <= 2), or ``split``.
AuditOut.title: AuditOut

ClaimDetailOut.description:
Full verification report.

Slim top-level for the 80% of callers who only need verdict + sources.
Audit detail lives under ``audit``.
ClaimDetailOut.title: ClaimDetailOut

DebateSideOut.description: One side of the debate (pro or con). Nested under ``audit``.
DebateSideOut.title: DebateSideOut

SourceOut.description: A research source used to evaluate a claim.
SourceOut.title: SourceOut

RelatedVerificationsOut.description:
Wrapper for ``GET /verifications/{id}/related``.

Empty ``items`` when the verification has no embedding yet (early
pipeline) or no public-library claim is close enough.
RelatedVerificationsOut.title: RelatedVerificationsOut

SimilarVerificationOut.description:
An existing public verification that semantically resembles a
given claim text.

Surfaced in two places:

* When duplicate detection finds a close-enough match on a fresh
  `/verify` (the ``duplicate_found`` needs-input branch).
* As an item in ``GET /verifications/{id}/related`` for callers
  surfacing "see also" references next to a verdict.
SimilarVerificationOut.title: SimilarVerificationOut

CandidateClaimOut.description:
A single candidate claim surfaced when the framing detects multiple
distinct claims in the submitted text. The caller picks one via
``POST /verify/{task_id}/select``.
CandidateClaimOut.title: CandidateClaimOut

NeedsInputOut.description:
Inline body for webhook ``event=verification.needs_input``.

The pipeline pauses for caller input when framing splits the text into
multiple distinct claims (``multi_claim``), needs disambiguation
(``clarification_required``), or finds a near-duplicate of an existing
verification (``duplicate_found``). Only one of ``claims``,
``candidate_claims``, or ``similar_claims`` is populated per event, keyed
on ``reason``. Resolve by calling ``POST /verify/{task_id}/select``.
NeedsInputOut.title: NeedsInputOut

WebhookPayload.description:
Body the customer's endpoint receives when a verification terminates.

Always signed with HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body bytes; signature
rides in the ``X-Lenz-Signature: sha256=<hex>`` header.

The ``attempt`` field is 1-indexed and includes the initial delivery,
so values run 1 through 4 (initial + 3 retries).
WebhookPayload.title: WebhookPayload

post.description: Fires when a verification reaches a terminal state with a verdict.

2XX.description: Your endpoint should return 2xx within 5 seconds. Non-2xx triggers retries at 10s / 60s / 600s (3 retries after the initial delivery, 4 attempts total).

post.description: Fires when the verification pipeline failed and no verdict is available.

2XX.description: Your endpoint should return 2xx within 5 seconds. Non-2xx triggers retries at 10s / 60s / 600s (3 retries after the initial delivery, 4 attempts total).

post.description: Fires when framing detects multiple claims, ambiguity, or a duplicate.
